questions.question: A convex mirror always produces an image that is:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always produce virtual, upright, and diminished images, regardless of object position.

questions.question: Compared to the object, the image formed by a convex mirror is always:
questions.explanation: The image in a convex mirror is always smaller than the object. Magnification is always less than 1.

questions.question: The focal point of a convex mirror is located:
questions.explanation: The focal point of a convex mirror is virtual — it lies behind the mirror where reflected rays appear to diverge from.

questions.question: A convex mirror is also called a diverging mirror because it:
options: Brings parallel rays to a focus
Reflects rays in a single direction
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors spread parallel incident rays outward, making them appear to diverge from a point behind the mirror.

questions.question: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles because they:
options: Produce magnified images of vehicles behind
Provide a wider field of view
Produce real images of approaching vehicles
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors show a wider area than plane mirrors, helping drivers see more of the road behind. The tradeoff is a diminished image.

questions.question: When an object is at infinity from a convex mirror, the image is formed at:
options: The center of curvature behind the mirror
The focal point behind the mirror
questions.explanation: Parallel rays from a distant object appear to diverge from the focal point behind the mirror — a virtual image at F.

questions.question: When parallel light rays strike a convex mirror parallel to its principal axis, they:
options: Converge directly at the real focal point in front of the mirror.
Diverge outwards as if originating from the focal point behind the mirror.
Reflect perfectly parallel to each other back toward the incoming light source.
Converge directly at the center of curvature in front of the mirror.
questions.explanation: A convex mirror is a diverging mirror. When parallel light rays hit its curved outer surface, they bounce away from each other. If an observer traces these separating rays backward behind the mirror, the virtual line extensions intersect at the mirror's virtual focal point.

questions.question: A convex mirror has a radius of curvature of $30\text{ cm}$. What is the magnitude of its focal length?
Convex mirrors are used in stores for surveillance because they:
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors curve outward, causing reflected light rays to diverge. This diverging behavior allows the mirror to capture light from a much broader perimeter, providing a wider field of view than a flat or concave mirror can manage in the same amount of space.

questions.question: In a convex mirror, no matter where the object is placed, the virtual image is always located:
options: Between the pole and the focal point behind the mirror surface.
Between the focal point and center of curvature behind the mirror.
Beyond the center of curvature on the front side of the mirror.
Beyond the real focal point on the front side of the mirror.
questions.explanation: Because a convex mirror always causes light rays to diverge upon reflection, the reflected rays must be traced backward behind the mirror's surface to find where they intersect. For any real object location in front of the mirror, these virtual lines will always converge within the confined boundary between the pole (P) and the virtual focal point (F).

questions.question: Which statement correctly summarizes image formation by a convex mirror?
options: Real, inverted, and can be magnified or diminished
questions.explanation: Convex mirrors always produce virtual, upright, and diminished images — a simple and consistent rule.

questions.question: Unlike a concave mirror, a convex mirror:
questions.explanation: A convex mirror never forms a real image — its images are always virtual. Concave mirrors can produce both real and virtual images.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered convex mirrors and their applications!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the virtual focal point and why images are always diminished.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on the consistent image properties of convex mirrors.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: convex mirrors always produce virtual, upright, diminished images behind the mirror.
